raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a formal complaint against Barclaycard 's Jetblue Credit Card for their multi-layered failure to properly handle my credit card dispute ( Case # XXXX ), resulting in a financial loss of {$920.00}. 

My initial dispute, dated XX/XX/year>, for a canceled hotel stay was incorrectly denied on XX/XX/XXXX ( attached ). 

As per their instructions, I filed a formal appeal on XX/XX/year>, and provided new, definitive evidence : a written statement from the hotel confirming they had already refunded the merchant 's third-party partner. The hotel also provided their direct phone number in case Barclaycard wished to contact them ( evidence attached ). Barclaycard ignored this conclusive evidence and upheld their original decision without justification. 

Following the failure of my appeal, in the second week of XXXX I formally requested XXXX XXXX for a second time. A Barclaycard representative acknowledged receipt of my request. 

It has now been nearly three weeks since I requested arbitration and over six weeks since my appeal and Barclaycard has taken no action, provided no updates, and sent no communication.

This demonstrates a complete breakdown in Barclaycard 's dispute resolution obligations. They have not only ignored conclusive evidence during my appeal but have also failed to act on my formal request for arbitration. By doing so, they have enabled the merchant to wrongfully keep my {$920.00} and have denied me access to the final stage of the dispute process.
